challenges.title: Column Width and Gap
challenges.description: Write CSS that uses `column-width` for a responsive multi-column layout. Create a rule for `.content` that sets `column-width: 250px` and `column-gap: 2em`. The browser will automatically determine the number of columns based on the available width. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'column-width' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-width property'
assert 'column-gap' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-gap property'
assert '250px' in css.lower(), 'Should set column-width to 250px'
assert '.content' in css.lower(), 'Should target .content'
hints: column-width sets the ideal width for each column; the browser calculates how many fit
Unlike column-count, column-width creates a responsive layout automatically

challenges.title: Preventing Column Breaks and Column Fill
challenges.description: Write CSS for a polished multi-column layout that prevents awkward breaks. Create a rule for `.multi-col` that sets `column-count: 3`, `column-gap: 30px`, and `column-fill: balance` to evenly distribute content. Create a rule for `.multi-col .card` that sets `break-inside: avoid` to prevent cards from being split across columns, and add `margin-bottom: 1em`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'break-inside' in css.lower(), 'Should use break-inside property'
assert 'avoid' in css.lower(), 'Should set break-inside to avoid'
assert 'column-fill' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-fill property'
assert 'balance' in css.lower(), 'Should set column-fill to balance'
assert 'column-count' in css.lower(), 'Should use column-count'
assert '.multi-col' in css.lower(), 'Should target .multi-col'
assert '.card' in css.lower(), 'Should target .card within .multi-col'
hints: break-inside: avoid prevents an element from being split across column boundaries
column-fill: balance distributes content evenly; column-fill: auto fills columns sequentially
